seo.title: RabbitMQ Performance Analytics — Historical Charts & Trends | Qarote
seo.description: Historical charts for RabbitMQ message throughput, queue depth trends, and broker resource usage. Spot capacity problems before they become incidents. No Prometheus or Grafana required.

hero.title: Understand your broker's past to plan its future.
hero.subtitle: Message throughput, queue depth trends, and broker resource usage — charted over time with configurable windows. Spot seasonal patterns, detect slow degradation, and answer 'was this always like this?' without standing up a Prometheus stack.

chartTypes.heading: Every metric that matters over time

items.title: Message throughput
items.description: Publish rate and deliver rate over time, per queue and broker-wide. Throughput charts are the first thing you reach for in a post-mortem — they tell you when message flow changed and by how much.

items.title: Queue depth trends
items.description: Message count per queue over configurable time windows. A queue that grows by 5% each day doesn't look alarming in real time — but a week of depth trend data makes the trajectory obvious before it becomes a capacity problem.

items.title: Broker memory usage
items.description: Memory consumption per node over time. RabbitMQ's memory alarm triggers at a configurable threshold — analytics let you see how close you're running and project when current usage will trigger an alarm.

items.title: Disk space trends
items.description: Disk usage per node over time. Disk alarms block publishing completely. Trend data lets you project runway — so you're adding storage on your schedule, not during an incident.

items.title: Consumer utilization
items.description: Consumer count and utilization rate over time. Consumer count drops correlate with queue depth spikes — seeing both on the same time axis is essential for reconstructing what happened during an incident.

items.title: Connection and channel history
items.description: Open connections and channels per broker over time. A connection count that grows 10% each week is a leak. Trend data makes the pattern unmistakable in a way that real-time metrics cannot.

howItWorks.heading: Charts built automatically from your metrics

steps.title: Connect your broker
steps.description: Qarote starts collecting metrics the moment you connect. No scrape configuration, no retention policy to set — collection is automatic.

steps.title: Select any queue or server
steps.description: Open any queue or broker node and switch to the analytics view. Charts render immediately from stored metric history — no query language needed.

steps.title: Adjust the time window
steps.description: Select 1 hour, 6 hours, 24 hours, or 7 days. Zoom into an incident window or review the full week's trend. The same chart, different resolution.

vsPrometheus.heading: Analytics without the infrastructure
vsPrometheus.p1: Prometheus with the `rabbitmq_prometheus` plugin gives you powerful analytics — if you're willing to operate a Prometheus server, configure scrape jobs, tune retention settings, and build Grafana dashboards from scratch. The typical setup takes half a day and produces dashboards that require ongoing maintenance as your queue topology changes.
vsPrometheus.p2: Qarote's analytics are built in. Queue depth, throughput, and resource charts are available immediately after connecting your broker — for every queue, automatically, with no dashboard to build. When you add a new queue, it appears in analytics the next time metrics are collected.
vsPrometheus.p3: The tradeoff is flexibility — Qarote's charts cover the metrics RabbitMQ operators actually use. If you need custom PromQL queries or cross-system correlation, Prometheus is the right tool. If you need RabbitMQ analytics that work in five minutes without infrastructure, Qarote is.

items.question: How far back do analytics go?
items.answer: Analytics charts draw from Queue History data — 7 days of snapshots at 5-minute intervals on Developer and Enterprise plans. The Community edition shows real-time metrics only.

items.question: Can I export chart data?
items.answer: Chart data export is available on Enterprise plans. Developer plans can view and screenshot charts in the dashboard. API access to metric history is an Enterprise feature.

items.question: Do I need to configure which metrics to collect?
items.answer: No. Qarote collects metrics from all queues on all connected servers automatically. There's no configuration, no metric selection, and no need to opt queues in individually.

items.question: How is this different from Queue History?
items.answer: Analytics and Queue History draw from the same underlying data — 5-minute snapshots stored for 7 days. Queue History emphasizes the raw timeline for incident retrospectives. Analytics emphasizes trend visualization, comparative charting, and capacity planning views over the same data.

items.question: Does analytics work with self-hosted Qarote?
items.answer: Yes. Self-hosted Qarote (Developer or Enterprise license) stores metric history in your PostgreSQL database. Analytics charts render from that local data — no cloud dependency.
